---
title: RStudio HPC Cluster Tutorial
editor: visual
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    toc: true
    toc-location: left
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-floating: true
execute:
  echo: true
  message: true
  warning: true
---

## Introduction

This tutorial explains how to use RStudio on the Leo5 high-performance computing (HPC) cluster from the University of Innsbruck. RStudio is the most widely used tool for working with R code and can also be installed on a local computer. However, some R tools require high compute power and storage, which is why using an HPC cluster can be necessary. Usually, HPC clusters are operated through terminal commands. For this tutorial, we need the graphical RStudio interface, so a special network protocol (X11) is required. This means additional setup steps on some operating systems.

## Requirements

To connect to the Leo5 HPC cluster, you need access to the university network. This means you are either physically on campus and connected to the university network, or you establish a VPN connection from your local computer. Instructions for setting up the VPN connection are available here: [https://www.uibk.ac.at/de/zid/anleitungen-und-dokumentationen/datennetz/vpn/](https://www.uibk.ac.at/de/zid/anleitungen-und-dokumentationen/datennetz/vpn/).

On the Leo5 cluster, you must log in with dedicated user accounts. These accounts are not the same as accounts used for other university services such as LFU access. In this tutorial, the account `c9881023` is used. The password will be provided by the course instructors. Be careful when changing files and settings, because this account is shared with other participants.

## Connection by Operating System

### Windows

On Windows, the tool MobaXterm is used to connect with the required network protocol. Download `MobaXterm Home Edition v26.0 (Portable edition)` from: [https://mobaxterm.mobatek.net/download-home-edition.html](https://mobaxterm.mobatek.net/download-home-edition.html).

Extract the ZIP folder and run the included `.exe` file. Click `New Session` and then choose `SSH`. In the `Remote Host` field, enter `leo5.uibk.ac.at`, and in the `Username` field, enter `c9881023`. Then click `OK`. A connection to the cluster should now be established.

### Linux

On Linux, no additional tool is usually required for this network protocol. Open a terminal and run:

```bash
ssh -X c9881023@leo5.uibk.ac.at
```

A connection to the cluster should now be established.

### macOS

On macOS, the tool XQuartz is required to establish a connection with the needed network protocol. Download XQuartz here: [https://www.xquartz.org](https://www.xquartz.org).

Install the tool and restart your computer. Open XQuartz (`Applications -> Utilities -> XQuartz`) and then run the following command in Terminal:

```bash
ssh -Y c9881023@leo5.uibk.ac.at
```

A connection to the cluster should now be established.

## Starting RStudio

At this point, you should be connected to the cluster based on your operating system. Next, we want to start RStudio in a virtual container via the tool Singularity. Load it with the following command in the terminal:

```bash
module load singularityce/4.3.0-python-3.11.9-gcc-13.3.0-n5qceek
```

Then run this command to start RStudio in the Singularity container:

```bash
singularity exec -B /scratch /scratch/c102180/rstudio_202601+392+R433.sif rstudio
```

The graphical RStudio interface should now open in a new window.
